seo.title: Sell House As-Is Las Vegas | No Repairs Needed
seo.description: Sell your Las Vegas house as-is for cash. No repairs, no cleaning, no staging. Fair cash offer in 24 hours. Close in 7-14 days.
keywords: sell house without repairs Las Vegas

hero.heading: Sell Your Las Vegas House As-Is for Cash
hero.subheading: No repairs. No cleaning. No staging. We buy it exactly the way it is.

sections.heading: What 'As-Is' Really Means When We Buy Your Home
sections.content:
When we say we buy houses as-is, we mean it literally. We've purchased Las Vegas homes with:

• Active roof leaks and major water damage
• Foundation cracks and structural issues
• Fire and smoke damage
• Mold and pest infestations
• Full of furniture, belongings, and years of accumulated items
• Code violations and permit issues
• Non-functioning HVAC, plumbing, or electrical systems
• Squatters or problem tenants in place

None of this stops us from making you a fair cash offer. We account for all condition issues in our valuation — you don't spend a dollar on repairs, and you're under no obligation to clean or stage the property.

items.title: Zero Repairs Required
items.description: Don't spend $10,000–$50,000+ getting a property ready for market. We buy it exactly as it stands today.

items.title: No Cleaning or Staging
items.description: Leave anything you don't want. We handle all cleanout and property prep after the purchase.

items.title: Sell in Days, Not Months
items.description: Skip the 60–120 day traditional listing process. We can close in as little as 7 days.

items.title: No Agent Commissions
items.description: Save the 5–6% you'd pay a listing agent. Our offer is what you receive — no deductions.

items.title: No Deal-Killing Inspections
items.description: Cash purchases don't require buyer inspections that can kill deals at the last minute.

items.title: Close on Your Timeline
items.description: 7 days or 60 days — you choose when to close. We work around your moving schedule.

sections.heading: The Hidden Cost of Making Repairs Before Selling
sections.html:
Many homeowners believe they need to repair their Las Vegas home before selling in order to get a fair price. But the math often tells a different story:

What Repairs Actually Cost

  • New roof: $8,000–$18,000
  • HVAC replacement: $5,000–$12,000
  • Foundation repair: $10,000–$50,000+
  • Kitchen remodel: $15,000–$40,000
  • Full paint and flooring: $8,000–$20,000
  • Water damage remediation: $3,000–$15,000

Beyond the dollars, repairs take time — usually weeks to months to plan, hire contractors, complete, and inspect. During that time, you're still paying mortgage, HOA, utilities, and insurance.

The As-Is Cash Sale Math

A cash buyer will offer less than full retail value, but when you subtract repair costs, carrying costs during the listing period, agent commissions (5–6%), and buyer concessions, the net difference is often surprisingly small — sometimes the cash sale puts more money in your pocket.

Request a free offer from us and run the numbers yourself. We're transparent about how we arrive at our offer, and there's zero obligation to accept.

sections.heading: Types of Properties We Buy As-Is
sections.html:
We have experience purchasing Las Vegas properties in every type of distressed or as-is condition:

  • Fire-damaged homes: We buy properties with structural fire damage, smoke damage, and incomplete repairs from prior fire claims.
  • Water-damaged homes: Flood damage, roof leaks, burst pipes — we assess and make offers regardless of extent.
  • Homes with foundation issues: Cracks, settlement, slab issues — we work with properties that conventional lenders won't touch.
  • Hoarder houses: Properties packed full of belongings. You take what you want; we handle the rest.
  • Rental properties with tenants: Difficult tenants, lease violations, unpaid rent — we purchase with tenants in place.
  • Code violation properties: Unpermitted additions, code violations, building department notices — we navigate these issues.
  • Outdated properties: Homes with 1980s–1990s interiors, old electrical panels, galvanized plumbing, or original kitchens and baths.
  • Vacant properties: Long-vacant homes with deferred maintenance, vandalism, or squatter issues.

sections.heading: Get Your As-Is Cash Offer Today
sections.content: No repairs needed. No fees. No obligation. Just a fair offer from a licensed Nevada real estate professional.

faqs.question: Do I really not need to make any repairs?
faqs.answer: Correct. When we say as-is, we mean as-is. You don't repair, clean, paint, or remove anything you don't want to. We factor the property's current condition into our offer. We've bought homes in every imaginable state — from minor cosmetic issues to serious structural damage.

faqs.question: Will a property in bad condition get a very low offer?
faqs.answer: Our offer reflects the property's current market value minus the cost of any work needed to bring it to a sellable condition. While yes, a home needing $50,000 in repairs will receive a lower offer than a move-in-ready home, you need to weigh that against what you'd spend on repairs, agent commissions, closing costs, and carrying costs in a traditional sale — the net difference is often much smaller than you'd expect.

faqs.question: Do you need to inspect the property before making an offer?
faqs.answer: We do a brief walkthrough to assess the property's condition. This usually takes 15–30 minutes. Unlike a buyer's inspection, this is our evaluation — not a contingency that can later kill the deal. Once we make an offer, we honor it.

faqs.question: What if there are code violations or permit issues?
faqs.answer: Code violations, unpermitted additions, and building department holds are something we deal with regularly in Las Vegas. These issues complicate a traditional sale significantly — financed buyers typically can't purchase properties with open code violations. We can. We factor these issues into our offer and handle them after the purchase.

faqs.question: Can I leave items behind that I don't want?
faqs.answer: Yes. Take what you want and leave the rest. Furniture, appliances, personal belongings, junk — we handle all cleanout after the purchase. This is one of the major conveniences of an as-is sale.

faqs.question: Is selling as-is for cash a good idea?
faqs.answer: It depends on your situation. If your home needs significant repairs, your timeline is tight, or you want certainty (no deals falling through due to financing or inspection contingencies), an as-is cash sale is often the most practical and financially smart choice. We're happy to walk you through the numbers so you can compare your options.

testimonials.name: Tom H.
testimonials.text: The house had serious water damage and my contractor quoted me $35,000 in repairs. I didn't have the money or the time. Casey made me a fair offer and I walked away in two weeks. Couldn't have asked for a better outcome given the circumstances.

testimonials.name: Betty M.
testimonials.text: My mom's house was full of 40 years of belongings. I dreaded the thought of cleaning it out before listing. Alchemy bought it as-is and I just took the things that mattered to me. They handled everything else. Total lifesaver.
